keywordspec.name: lge.auto.SSHManager
keywordspec.doc: usage: >>> import SSHManager >>> ssh_manager = SSHManager() >>> ssh_manager.create_ssh_client(hostname, username, password) >>> ssh_manager.send_command("ls -al") >>> ssh_manager.send_file("/path/to/local_path", "/path/to/remote_path") >>> ssh_manager.get_file("/path/to/remote_path", "/path/to/local_path") ... >>> ssh_manager.close_ssh_client()

kw.name: Get file list in directory
kw.doc: Get file list in directory with path
kw.shortdoc: Get file list in directory with path

kw.name: Get File via ssh
kw.doc: Get a single file from remote path
kw.shortdoc: Get a single file from remote path

kw.name: Send File via ssh
kw.doc: Send a single file to remote path
kw.shortdoc: Send a single file to remote path

type.name: list
type.doc:
Strings must be Python [https://docs.python.org/library/stdtypes.html#list|list] literals. They are converted to actual lists using the [https://docs.python.org/library/ast.html#ast.literal_eval|ast.literal_eval] function. They can contain any values ``ast.literal_eval`` supports, including lists and other containers.
If the type has nested types like ``list[int]``, items are converted to those types automatically. This in new in Robot Framework 6.0.
Examples: ``['one', 'two']``, ``[('one', 1), ('two', 2)]``

type.name: string
type.doc: All arguments are converted to Unicode strings.
